name: Ketterdam RPG
description:
No Mourners, No Funerals

The fog rolls thick off Ketterdam's harbor tonight, shrouding the canals in a ghostly mist. Lanterns cast their colored glow across the cobblestones - red for the pleasure houses, green for the gambling dens, gold for the upscale establishments where merchers make their deals. The city never truly sleeps, merely shifts from one scheme to the next as day bleeds into darkness.

In the Barrel, the city's beating black heart, the scent of salt water mingles with pipe smoke and the sweet tang of jurda. Crowds move between betting parlors and bars, each person carrying their own secrets and desperate dreams. Music spills from the Crow Club's open doors, while in darker alleys, shadows conduct business with glinting knives and whispered bargains.

The night is young in this city of infinite possibility and lurking danger. Here, fortunes rise and fall between sunset and sunrise, every double-cross has its price and every debt comes due.

In Ketterdam's twisted streets, a your story is about to unfold. Who are you and what are you doing?

.........

Wheeew, I've been tweaking with this thing for a week and I'm going to go ahead and post it but consider it in Beta. I plan on continuously updating this as context improves and I learn more about what works and what doesnt. And that is the intro message above!

Currently the cast consists of Kaz Brekker, Inej Ghafa, Jesper Fahey, Wylan Van Eck, and Nina Zenik since this follows Crooked Kingdom canon. I might add some OCs as other gang leaders because the bot does have trouble remembering who it says is in charge of the other gangs after while since I did not specify anything.

I have a Kaz bot coming soon as well and might end up doing all of the Crows (including Matthias).

........

Tips for getting the most out of the bot:

Use the Chat Memory. For example, if you want to leader of a new gang, put the gang information in as briefly as possible. If you're playing a character who is friends with the cast, this is where you would let the bot know.

Be detailed. The less you give the bot, the more likely it will fill in the blanks by describing what you're doing. Remember, you're writing WITH the bot, not against it.

I totally focused more on the Barrel and the gangs than anything else to the point I should probably call it the Barrel RPG. (In fact, it might eventually become that.)
creator: kittylace
